unmatchedNotices.title: Boonanghi State Forest: Closed

unmatchedNotices.title: Boyben State Forest: 1080 baiting for pest animal control
unmatchedNotices.detailText:
Manufactured 1080 dog baits will be laid within Boyben State Forest between 1 June 2026 and 16 June 2026.
Pets and working dogs should be restrained or muzzled to avoid poisoning. If accidental ingestion does occur, please seek immediate veterinary assistance.
For further information regarding this program, please call 0429 413 669.

structuredImpact.rationale: Notice only mentions 1080 baiting and pet safety, no access or camping restrictions stated.

unmatchedNotices.title: Carrai State Forest: Closed
unmatchedNotices.detailText:

Please follow signage on the site and follow any direction from Forestry Corporation staff.
Entry into closed areas without the permission of Forestry Corporation is prohibited and can lead to prosecution (maximum penalty $2,200 per offence).
Please note that Carrai State Forest remains closed over the longer term due to significant impact from severe weather and bushfires.

unmatchedNotices.title: Nerong State Forest closed
unmatchedNotices.detailText:
Recent flooding and sever weather impacts have resulted in
temporary closures of State Forests in the Hunter and Central Coast Areas. This includes Masseys Creek State Forest.
Forestry Corporation staff are continuing to undertake work
and assessments to clear roads and make areas safe for visitation. Forests will
progressively reopen once they are made safe and accessible.

unmatchedNotices.title: Northern NSW State forests: 1080 baiting for pest animal control
unmatchedNotices.detailText:
Forestry Corporation, through a program delivered by Local Land Services, will be conducting aerial application of 1080 baits targeting wild dogs and foxes in northern NSW State forests from 18 May to 19 July 2026.
Treated areas will be signposted. We strongly advise that if you intend to bring a companion dog with you, that you pay attention to warning signs marking baited areas, restrain your dogs to ensure they do not enter the signposted areas, or muzzle your dogs if entering these areas.
In the event of accidental poisoning, seek immediate veterinary assistance.
The following State forests are included in the pest control program:
Bald Knob
Barrington Tops
Barrington Tops

unmatchedNotices.title: Putty State Forest: partly closed
unmatchedNotices.detailText:
Please note that areas pf Putty State Forest remains closed over the longer term due to significant impact from severe weather and bushfires.
Putty State Forest consists of two areas. One is managed by Forestry Corporation, the other, a Flora Reserve, managed by the National Parks and Wildlife Service.
This advisory refers only to the area managed by Forestry Corporation - a defined area generally the eastern part of Putty State Forest.
Commission Road and a section of Main Hunter Trail are currently opened. All other roads, tracks and fire trails including their parts within the defined area remain closed.
For information on roads outside the definite area, within Flora Reserve, please contact the National Parks and Wildlife Service.

structuredImpact.rationale: Most roads and tracks in the Forestry Corporation area are closed, limiting vehicle access and camping.

unmatchedNotices.title: Turon State Forest: 1080 baiting for pest animal control
unmatchedNotices.detailText:
Forestry Corporation will be deploying ground application of 1080 baits targeting wild dogs and foxes in Turon State Forest from 4-8 May 2026.
There is a six-week timeframe during which the 1080 will be effective (4 May - 15 June). All entrances to these areas will be signposted.
Caution should be exercised with domestic animals within the baited areas.
We strongly advise that if you intend to bring a companion dog, you:
pay attention to warning signs marking baited areas
restrain your dogs to ensure they do not enter the signposted areas, or
muzzle your dogs if entering these areas.
In the event of accidental poisoning, seek immediate veterinary assistance.

closureNotices.title: Hampton State Forest: 1080 baiting for pest animal control
closureNotices.detailText:
Forestry Corporation will be deploying ground application of 1080 baits targeting wild dogs and foxes in Hampton State Forest from 4-8 May 2026.
There is a six-week timeframe during which the 1080 will be effective (4 May - 15 June). All entrances to these areas will be signposted.

We strongly advise that if you intend to bring a companion dog, you:
pay attention to warning signs marking baited areas
restrain your dogs to ensure they do not enter the signposted areas, or
muzzle your dogs if entering these areas.
In the event of accidental poisoning, seek immediate veterinary assistance.

structuredImpact.rationale: The notice advises caution and restraint of dogs but does not indicate any closures or restrictions to camping or vehicle access.

closureNotices.title: Pennsylvania State Forest: Road closures (see notice for details)
closureNotices.detailText:
Please note the following updates to road closures and openings in Pennsylvania and Roseberg State Forests:
Tea Tree road into Roseberg State Forest is closed. It will remain closed till approximately the end of May 2026.
All other forest roads within Pennsylvania State Forest will remain open until future harvest operation start. Caution is advised as we still have active haulage in the area, running through to Roseburg State Forest along Colo and Carrollina Roads.

structuredImpact.rationale: Tea Tree road is closed, restricting access, but other roads remain open with caution advised due to active haulage; camping impact is not specified.

closureNotices.title: Sunny Corner State Forest: 1080 baiting for pest animal control
closureNotices.detailText:
Forestry Corporation will be deploying ground application of 1080 baits targeting wild dogs and foxes in Sunny Corner State Forest from 4-8 May 2026.
There is a six-week timeframe during which the 1080 will be effective (4 May - 15 June). All entrances to these areas will be signposted.
Caution should be exercised with domestic animals within the baited areas.
We strongly advise that if you intend to bring a companion dog, you:
pay attention to warning signs marking baited areas
restrain your dogs to ensure they do not enter the signposted areas, or
muzzle your dogs if entering these areas.
In the event of accidental poisoning, seek immediate veterinary assistance.

structuredImpact.rationale: No specific impact language detected.

closureNotices.title: Sunny Corner State Forest: Mary’s Park closed for restoration
closureNotices.detailText: Please note that Mary's Park in Sunny Corner State Forest is closed for restoration.

structuredImpact.rationale: Partial closure status implies at least some access restrictions.

closureNotices.title: Vulcan State Forest: Blue Hill quarry area closed to fossicking
closureNotices.detailText:
Please note Blue Hill Quarry and the area surrounding it within Vulcan State Forest is currently closed to fossicking while a safety assessment is conducted.
We encourage all fossickers to understand and adhere to the conditions of their permit and relevant legislation.
Please note that all quarries are always closed to the public.
Further information on fossicking within State forests is available here.
Contact: Statewide Information Centre 1300 655 687

structuredImpact.rationale: Partial closure status implies at least some access restrictions.

closureNotices.title: Bago State Forest: Sections of plantations closed
closureNotices.detailText:
Camping areas at Blowering dam are open.
Please note that sections of plantations in Bago State Forest, west of Blowering Dam, remain closed due to significant impact from severe weather and bushfires.
Main access roads to Blowering Dam are open and safe. However minor roads remain closed and are generally inaccessible.
Please follow any road signage and pay attention to conditions when driving.

structuredImpact.rationale: Camping areas at Blowering Dam are open but some plantation sections are closed; main roads are open but minor roads are closed.

closureNotices.title: Bellangry State Forest: Number 1 Tower Picnic Area closed
closureNotices.detailText:
Number 1 Tower Picnic Area has been closed due to rehabilitation works taking place.
There is no access to Number 1 Tower as this work was scheduled, but has had to take place earlier than expected due to the grassed areas being extensively damaged over the Easter Weekend.
Visitors are asked to adhere to signage, barricades and any direction given from any Forestry Corporation Staff and to avoid the area at all times.
Contact: Statewide Information Centre 1300 655 687

structuredImpact.rationale: The Number 1 Tower Picnic Area is closed with no access allowed due to rehabilitation works.

closureNotices.title: Bondo State Forest: Partial Road Closure Notice - Nottingham Road
closureNotices.detailText:
Due to the structural damage of a log bridge over Oaks Creek, Nottingham Road will remain closed between Brindabella Road and Ridge Road until further notice.
Road closure signs will be in place for the affected section of road and traffic will be detoured via Nottingham Road, Ridge Road and Brindabella Road.

structuredImpact.rationale: Nottingham Road is closed between Brindabella Road and Ridge Road due to bridge damage, restricting vehicle access; camping impact is not mentioned.

closureNotices.title: Boorabee State Forest: Partial closure
closureNotices.detailText:
Please note that parts of Boorabee State Forest will be closed from 9:00 am Monday 20 April 2026 to 6:00 pm Friday 1 January 2027 for roading and harvesting activity.
The Kyogle MTB Club tracks in the area will be closed while these operations are active.
Please follow signage on the site and any direction from Forestry Corporation staff.
Download the document below for a detailed map of the closed area.
Entry into closed areas without the permission of Forestry Corporation is prohibited and can lead to prosecution (maximum penalty $2,200 per offence).

structuredImpact.rationale: Parts of the forest are closed for roading and harvesting, restricting access; no specific mention of camping impact.

closureNotices.title: Chichester State Forest: Partial closures and vehicle load limits
closureNotices.detailText:
Parts of Chichester State Forest are inaccessible and closed
for all visitors until further notice due to damage and various hazards
including landslips, following extreme weather events.
Road closures and vehicle load limits at Allyn River visitor sites:
Mount
Allyn Forest Road – Closed (at Shellbrook Road) – No
access to Mount Allyn Lookout
Allyn
River Forest Road – Load Limits – several bridges on
Allyn River Forest Road have load and speed limit restrictions. A bridge
north of Ladies Well has a max load limit of 5 Tonnes. Please
follow any signage and directions in place when travelling this area.
Please note: All camping and picnic/day-use sites are
open unless mentioned above.
Forestry Corporation staff are continuing to undertake work
to make areas safe for visitation and thank everyone for their patience.
Last reviewed: 4 June 2026.

structuredImpact.rationale: Some roads and bridges are closed or have load limits, but camping sites remain open.

closureNotices.title: Coopernook State Forest: Increased truck traffic
closureNotices.detailText:
Timber harvesting will commence in Coopernook State Forest from 3 February until further notice.
Parts of this operation will take place in close proximity to Coopernook HQ Camping Ground. However, access to the popular camping area will remain open.
There will be an increase in traffic due to harvesting activities, with haulage trucks using visitor access routes, including Juhles Mountain Road, Langley Vale Way, Bangalow Road, and particularly Forest Road, which runs parallel to the camping area.
View a map of the affected roads PDF, 370.04 KB
Visitors are asked to:
be vigilant with children and animals
follow all signage, barricades and directions from Forestry Corporation staff
avoid the harvesting operation area at all times.

structuredImpact.rationale: Camping remains open but increased truck traffic and safety advisories are in place.

closureNotices.title: Kerewong State Forest: Temporary closure of Kerewong Road
closureNotices.detailText:
Harvesting will commence in Kerewong State Forest northwest of Swans Crossing Camping area from 17 March until further notice. This may impact tourists and members of the public.
The Swans Crossing Camping area will remain open, as will Upsalls Creek Road access, however the alternate route of Kerewong Road heading south from Comboyne Tower will be closed for the duration of this harvesting operation.
Please observe all signs and any direction given by Forestry staff. These measures are for the safety of road users, contractors, pedestrians and forest users.
Thank you for your patience.

structuredImpact.rationale: Camping area remains open; Kerewong Road closed but alternate routes open, restricting vehicle access.

closureNotices.title: Penrose State Forest closed - Limited camping only off Paddys River Road
closureNotices.detailText:
Penrose State Forests has been closed due to serious concerns for visitor safety.
Hazardous road conditions, created by vehicles using roads during wet weather, forced this closure.
Find out more about visiting sustainably.
Restoration work has commenced however it is extensive and weather-dependent. Visitors can access the designated camp areas via the advised routes.
Low impact recreational activities (non-vehicle) are permitted in the closed forest. Locally Wingello State Forest has partially reopened and is an option for vehicle recreational activities. Observe all signage and please visit sustainably.
A 400m area off Paddys River Road will remain open for camping.
Access only via Penrose Forest Way. Nearest facilities are located at Kingsbury VC rest area.
Visitors may park safety off Penrose Forest Way.

structuredImpact.rationale: Camping is limited to a 400m area off Paddys River Road and access is only via Penrose Forest Way; hazardous road conditions have caused partial closure.

closureNotices.title: Wild Cattle Creek State Forest road closure - Moses Rock Road
closureNotices.detailText:
Moses Rock Road will be closed to the public for three days to facilitate tree removal works as part of a bridge replacement project in Wild Cattle Creek State Forest, west of Cascade.
Works will start on Wednesday, June 10, weather pending, and continue until Friday, June 12.
Replacement of the bridge over Cascade Creek is expected to commence later this year.
For further information contact the Coffs Harbour office on (02) 6652 0111

structuredImpact.rationale: Road/access closure language detected.
